program.name: Seattle Fresh Bucks
program.description_short: Monthly $60 benefit for Seattle residents to buy fruits and vegetables at participating stores
program.description:
Seattle Fresh Bucks is a city-funded healthy-food program that gives income-eligible Seattle households $60 each month to spend on fruits and vegetables. Benefits are loaded onto a Fresh Bucks card or the Healthy Benefits+ app and can be used at many participating Seattle farmers markets, independent grocers, and supermarkets.

Fresh Bucks currently uses a waitlist with randomized selection. Eligible households submit an application and are entered into a lottery; selected households are notified by mail and email when funding becomes available. Households with the lowest income, those where a language other than English is primarily spoken, and those who have been on the waitlist longer receive additional entries to increase their chance of selection.

There is one benefit per household. If anyone in your household is already enrolled in Fresh Bucks, additional applications will not be accepted.

Click 'Apply Now' to submit your application online. You will need your household size, monthly household income, current Seattle home address, and date of birth to complete it.
program.website_description: Monthly $60 benefit to buy fruits and vegetables at participating Seattle stores

warning_messages.message: Fresh Bucks currently uses a waitlist. Submitting an application places you on the waitlist; benefits are awarded through a randomized selection process when funding becomes available. Submitting multiple applications does not increase your chance of enrollment.
Only one Fresh Bucks benefit is allowed per household. If anyone in your household is already enrolled, additional applications will not be accepted.
You must live within Seattle city limits to qualify. Some King County ZIP codes straddle the city boundary, so the program will verify your exact street address when you apply.

navigators.name: Seattle Office of Sustainability & Environment - Fresh Bucks
navigators.description: Program staff at the Seattle Office of Sustainability & Environment can answer questions about Fresh Bucks eligibility, the waitlist application, and selection notifications. Available by phone Monday-Friday during business hours and by email anytime.
